question.text: Which of the following is NOT an input to the Black-Scholes option pricing model?

answer.text: The current spot price of the underlying

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The five inputs to Black-Scholes are: spot price, strike price, time to expiry, risk-free rate, and volatility. Trading volume is not an input. **The Trap:** All other choices are legitimate Black-Scholes inputs. Volume affects liquidity but not theoretical option pricing. **Cross-Exam Context:** Black-Scholes inputs are tested in NISM Equity Derivatives and Research Analyst modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the five inputs: S (spot), K (strike), T (time), r (risk-free rate), sigma (volatility). Any question adding a sixth input is likely the wrong answer.
